channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: JD Vance visits Italy for tariff talks and attends Good Friday service at the Vatican
item.description: ROME (AP) — U.S. Vice President JD Vance met with Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni in Rome on Friday for a second day of talks on tariffs and then attended Good Friday services at the Vatican amid speculation of a possible pope greeting this weekend.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Venice expands day-tripper tax in bid to combat overtourism
item.description: VENICE, Italy (AP) — Venice is charging day-trippers to the famed canal city an arrivals tax for the second year starting Friday, a measure aimed at combating the kind of overtourism that put the city's UNESCO World Cultural Heritage status at risk.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Worshippers return to restored Notre Dame for Easter rituals with emotion and awe
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Four months after its long-awaited reopening, Notre Dame Cathedral welcomed worshippers and tourists alike on Friday for an emotional Easter ceremony centered on the Crown of Thorns, a circular band of branches encased in a golden tube and one of Christianity’s most revered relics.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Chinese firm aiding Houthi attacks on US vessels, as airstrikes kill 74
item.description: The State Department is accusing a Chinese satellite firm of providing imagery to support Houthi rebel attacks in the Red Sea, escalating tensions amid ongoing maritime security concerns in the region.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US man hijacks small plane in Belize, stabs 3 people before he's shot dead
item.description: Authorities at the Philip S.W. Goldson International Airport reported a hijacking of a small plan by a U.S. man who stabbed two passengers and a pilot before being fatally shot.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Deadly US airstrike on Yemeni oil port escalates Trump's campaign against the Houthis
item.description: Yemen’s Houthi rebels say U.S. airstrikes targeting oil port killed at least 74 people and injured at least 171 others.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A recovering Pope Francis visits Rome prison to keep annual Holy Week appointment
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis visited Rome's central prison Thursday and met with dozens of inmates as he kept an Easter season appointment to spend Holy Thursday among the least fortunate, even as he continues recovering from a life-threatening bout of pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US to withdraw 600 troops from Syria, leaving fewer than 1,000 to help counter IS militants
item.description: The U.S. troops have been critical not only in the operations against the Islamic State but as a buffer for the Kurdish forces against Turkey.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cable car accident in southern Italy kills at least 4 people
item.description: MILAN (AP) — A cable car carrying tourists south of Naples crashed to the ground Thursday after a cable snapped, killing at least four people and critically injuring one, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Haitians face record hunger as gang violence grips country in throes of economic crisis
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — More than half of Haiti’s population is expected to experience severe hunger through June, and another 8,400 people living in makeshift shelters are projected to starve, according to a new report released this week.
item.dc:creator: EVENS SANON and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says US-Ukraine minerals deal could be signed this month
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he expects to sign a minerals agreement with Ukraine in the coming days, a few weeks after an expected deal signing was called off following a contentious Oval Office meeting with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, The Hill

item.title: ICC opens inquiry into Hungary for failing to arrest Netanyahu
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — Judges at the International Criminal Court want Hungary to explain why it failed to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu when he visited Budapest earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Noem threatens Harvard with ban on foreign student visas
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem threatened Wednesday to ban Harvard University from taking in foreign students amid a growing battle over the Ivy League school not accepting the demands of the Trump administration to change its policies.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: Former MLS player Aaron Boupendza dies after falling from building, soccer officials say
item.description: The Gabonese soccer federation announced Wednesday that Aaron Boupendza, a striker for its men's national team, has died after falling from the 11th floor of a building in China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A Russian bucket brigade helps toads and frogs cross the road to get to a spawning site
item.description: A NATURE RESERVE NEAR SESTRORETSK, Russia (AP) — It happens every spring along a section of road north of Russia's second-largest city of St. Petersburg: Volunteers, many in yellow vests, patrol near the Sestroretsk Bog nature reserve.
item.dc:creator: KIRILL ZARUBIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lebanon detains several people on suspicion of firing rockets at Israel
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Lebanese military said it has detained a group of people linked to firing rockets into Israel last month.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Italian prime minister meets with Trump on tariffs
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni is expected to speak for Italy and the European Union as a whole.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Carney says Trump key issue in Canada's election, while Conservative rival says country needs change
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Prime Minister Mark Carney said Wednesday the key question in Canada's election is who is best to deal with U.S. President Donald Trump while his Conservative rival argued that Carney doesn't represent change after a decade of Liberal Party rule.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dog reunites with Israeli family after disappearing for 18 months in Gaza
item.description: BINYAMINA, Israel (AP) — Rachel Dancyg never thought she would see her dog again after it disappeared in the Hamas attack that sparked the ongoing war with Israel. Her ex-husband and brother were abducted from Kibbutz Nir Oz in southern Israel and killed.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Puerto Rico goes dark after massive power outage
item.description: Puerto Rico went dark on Wednesday after experiencing a massive power outage on the island impacting families, businesses and homes alike.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: A Berlin doctor has been charged with the killings of 15 patients under palliative care
item.description: A German doctor has been charged with murdering over 15 people under palliative care; he has also been accused of attempting to cover up the crimes by starting fires in their homes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UK's top court says definition of a woman is based on biological sex and excludes transgender people
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The U.K. Supreme Court ruled Wednesday that a woman is someone born biologically female, excluding transgender people from the legal definition in a long-running dispute between a feminist group and the Scottish government.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, JILL LAWLESS and SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Top Trump officials will hold talks with Europeans on the Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Steve Witkoff, President Donald Trump’s special envoy, will travel to Paris this week for talks with European allies on U.S. efforts to negotiate a peace deal between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Soros v Trump: Socialists targets conservatives in upcoming European nation's election
item.description: Ahead of elections in May, Albania’s PM Edi Rama has been accused of targeting opposition candidates. One opposition figures says the influence of President Trump, George and Alex Soros will be felt.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shootout rescues Tennessee pastor abducted in South Africa
item.description: Josh Sullivan, a self-described “church-planting missionary,” has been reunited with his wife and their two children.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: American pastor kidnapped in South Africa rescued after deadly police shootout
item.description: American pastor Josh Sullivan, who was kidnapped from his church in the Eastern Cape Province last week, has been rescued by police following a "high-intensity shootout.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hong Kong post office will stop shipping parcels to US over tariffs
item.description: The U.S. earlier announced it would end a customs exception allowing small-value parcels from Hong Kong to enter the U.S. without tax.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NATO chief reaffirms support for Ukraine during a visit to the port city of Odesa
item.description: ODESA, Ukraine (AP) — NATO’s support for Ukraine remains “unwavering,” the alliance’s secretary-general said Tuesday, emphasizing that more than 20 billion euros — over $22 billion — in security assistance has already been pledged by NATO allies in the first three months of the year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hamas rejects Israeli ceasefire, hostage return deal over disarmament demands
item.description: Hamas has reportedly rejected Israel's latest ceasefire offer, which included the return of humanitarian aid in exchange for nearly a dozen hostages and Hamas' complete disarmament.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian general who criticized leadership may soon lead battalion of ex-cons: lawyer
item.description: Russia may soon reinstate a general who criticized Russia's top military leadership for blunders in Ukraine on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top Hamas terror leader killed in 'precise strike' by Israel: IDF
item.description: Top Hamas leader Muhammad al-‘Ajlah has been killed in an Israeli strike, the IDF has announced. Al-'Ajilah was responsible for arming terrorists with weapons used to carry out attacks against Israeli civilians and IDF troops, according to the IDF.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Father of American-Israeli hostage pushes for ceasefire
item.description: Adi Alexander shares a message for his son, urging him to stay strong and assuring him the family won’t stop fighting for his return.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas says it's sending a delegation to Qatar to continue Gaza ceasefire talks
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Hamas official said Monday that the Palestinian militant group is sending a delegation to the Gulf state of Qatar to continue indirect ceasefire talks with Israel over the war in Gaza, as the territory’s Health Ministry said that 38 people were confirmed dead over
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says Iran must ditch ‘concept of a nuclear weapon’ ahead of more talks
item.description: President Donald Trump says Iran "wants to deal" with Washington, but he once again demanded that the world's number one terror sponsor drop any "concept of a nuclear weapon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One US college student released after two detained in Denmark for alleged Uber incident
item.description: One of the two U.S. college students detained in Denmark over an alleged assault following an Uber ride has been released, according to a statement from his family.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, WGN 9

item.title: Daughter of German-American dissident whose body was mutilated by Iran calls on Trump to nix nuke talks
item.description: The daughter of a California man, Jamshid "Jimmy" Sharmahd, who was executed and mutilated by Iran, wants the U.S. to break off nuclear talks with the clerical regime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Universal announces UK theme park amid 'epic' growth of parks business
item.description: Universal Destinations & Experiences is expanding its global theme park footprint, confirming a new theme park will be built in the U.K. -- the latest in a trio of ambitious projects for the company.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, WFLA, NBC
